title: ‘The Batman’ and Batman Movie Rankings
format: Van Lathan and Rob Mahoney join to break down Matt Reeves's grounded, noir-soaked take on Batman, then rank all 14 live-action and animated Batman movies. Plus: Kogonada on his new film, After Yang.

segments.heading: ‘The Batman’
segments.note: Van Lathan and Rob Mahoney join to talk Matt Reeves's grounded, noir take

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Reeves's year-two Batman: bolted to the ground rather than merely grounded, no superpowers, barely any gadgets beyond a journal and some contact lenses. Both guests call it maybe the best-written Batman script yet, and dig into Robert Pattinson's performance (Rob's favorite live-action Batman, over Keaton), Zoe Kravitz's Catwoman, Paul Dano's terminally-online Riddler, and the mass-shooting set piece Van found genuinely hard to sit through. The mid-movie Joker reveal (Barry Keoghan) is the one point of real agreement between them: a cheap trick that deflates the ending.

films.title: Se7en
films.note: Van's comp for the movie's shape: essentially Se7en with Batman as the detective protagonist.

films.title: Bullitt
films.note: The reference point for the practical, no-CGI car chase, described as a 1970s muscle-car chase transplanted into a Batman movie.

films.title: Drive
films.note: Cited alongside Bullitt as the modern equivalent of that chase's aesthetic.

segments.heading: The Batman Movie Rankings
segments.note: all 14 live-action and animated films, best to worst
segments.subject: Batman movies

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: No. 1 - the movie every other entry gets measured against, including by Van, who isn't otherwise a Nolan fan.

films.title: Batman
films.note: No. 2 - Van and Sean's pick over Rob's active campaign for Batman Returns; the case for it is really the case for Jack Nicholson's Joker.

films.title: Batman Returns
films.note: No. 3 - Rob argued it should beat the 1989 film outright; Van and Sean wouldn't budge, but call it close.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 4 - ranked high off one viewing but deliberately kept behind the older films, to guard against recency bias before a rewatch.

films.title: Batman: Mask of the Phantasm
films.note: No. 5 - the group's animated pick; they like that it tells a full Batman story in 76 lean minutes.

films.title: Batman Begins
films.note: No. 6 - Van and Rob just don't respond to the Nolan trilogy's bookends the way Sean does.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: No. 7 - the weakest of the remaining live-action films for this group.

films.title: Batman Forever
films.note: No. 8 - Van's nostalgia pick, Happy Meal cup and all, dinged hard for Chris O'Donnell.

films.title: The Lego Batman Movie
films.note: No. 9 - Sean's veto pick; he clocks the joke hit rate at 5 to 7 percent for him.

films.title: Batman: The Movie
films.note: No. 10 - the Adam West version, fun for its first 30 minutes and unbeatable for the porpoise-saves-Batman gag.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: No. 11 - the four-hour Snyder Cut, watched specifically for this ranking since the theatrical Batman v Superman cut wasn't on HBO Max; a secondary Batman appearance either way.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: No. 12 - ranked by the Ultimate Edition; even the Ben Affleck truthers on the panel call the movie itself loathsome.

films.title: Justice League
films.note: No. 13 - the Joss Whedon theatrical cut, nearly tied with Batman and Robin for last.

films.title: Batman & Robin
films.note: No. 14 - unanimous worst pick, though Sean argues it's historically important as the flop that convinced studios to lock down franchise filmmaking.

films.title: After Yang
films.note: Kogonada's second feature, freshly in select theaters; Sean's favorite movie out of Sundance, an intimate sci-fi about a family and their malfunctioning android.

films.title: Columbus
films.note: His 2017 debut, shot in 18 days; discussed as the small-scale predecessor to After Yang's larger canvas.

films.title: Drive My Car
films.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi's film, the one that sent Kogonada down a rabbit hole into the director's earlier work.

films.title: Touching the Skin of Eeriness
films.note: An early, under-an-hour Hamaguchi work a friend sent him a link to; he says he's still trying to figure it out.

films.title: Arrival
films.note: Cited as evidence Denis Villeneuve is the kind of craftsman Kogonada admires.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Villeneuve's adaptation, which he says washed over him watching it in a great theater with his son.

films.title: In Bruges
films.note: Where Kogonada says he fell for Colin Farrell as a man more than as an actor.

films.title: Early Summer
films.note: The Ozu film he cites for its family-in-sync-before-it-dissolves rhythm, an influence on After Yang's opening dance sequence.

films.title: Kid with the Golden Arm
films.note: The Shaw Brothers film whose suspended, color-background title sequence he says directly inspired After Yang's own title sequence.

films: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
